title: practical english usage - Part 264
content:
The  ground floor of a British house is the first floor of an American house; 8  floors vocabulary •  322   numbers However, pence is often used instead of penny (a five-pence stamp).

Singular forms are used in expressions like a five-pound note (see above).

It cost me eight pound fifty.

That's two pounds and one pence, please.

sometimes used informally as a plural.

Some people now use the plural pence as a singular in informal speech; pound is seventy-five pence or £3.75  three pounds seventy-five (pence)   (more formal) three pounds and 5p five pence (informal five p) or 1 p one penny (informal one p (/pi:/)      a penny) There are 100  pence in a pound. Sums of money are named as follows: 15  British    money For the use of possessive forms in expressions of time (e.g. ten minutes' walk, four days journey), ►     124.5.

For the use of be in measurements, ►    25.2.

My father's just over six foot two.

especially when we  talk about people’ s heights.

In an informal style, we  often use foot instead of feet in other structures, a three-month-old baby six two-hour lessons    a six-foot tall man a three-mile walk     a four-foot deep hole not a five-pound note (     a five-pounds note) expressions.

Singular forms are used as modifiers before nouns in plural measuring millions of years - a few million years It cost thousands.

- several thousand times hundreds of pounds - five hundred pounds Compare: no final -s, and of is not used. This also happens after several and a few.

After a number, the words dozen, hundred, thousand, million and billion have 14 five     hundred,         etc   without      -s a million million.) A  billion is a thousand million. (But in older British usage a billion was 13 billion It was built in fifteen (hundred and) twenty-nine.

He was horn in thirteen hundred.

This form is used in historical dates before 2000 (►  324).

We only got fifteen hundred pounds for the car.

between 1,100  and 1,900.

one thousand one hundred, etc. This is most common with round numbers In an informal style we  often use eleven hundred, twelve hundred, etc instead of 12 eleven       hundred,         etc Vocabulary Areas  Section 29 vocabulary •  322   numbers or We're doing seventy miles an hour, (   ...  70 miles per hour / mph.) or It costs two pounds a week, (   ... £2  per week.) formal writing.

When we  relate two different measures, we  usually use a!an; per is often used in 19 a  and   per A  container 2 metres by 2 metres by 3 metres has a volume of 12 cubic metres.

is 144 square feet.

A  room twelve feet by twelve feet can be called twelve feet square; the total area thirty metres by forty-eight metres.

We   say, for example, that a room is twelve feet by fifteen feet, or that a garden is 18  area    and   volume 1 square mile = 640 acres = 259  hectares 1 acre = 4,840 square yards = 0.4 hectares 1 US gallon = 3.78 litres 1 British gallon = 4.55 litres 8 pints (8pt) - 1 gallon 1 US pint = 473ml 1 British pint = 568ml 14 pounds (14lb) = 1 stone (6.4kg) (BrE only) 2.2 pounds (2.2lb) = lkg 16 ounces - 1 pound (455gm) 1 ounce (loz) = 28gm 5 miles = 8km 5,280 feet / 1,760 yards = 1 mile (1.6km) 3 feet (3ft) = 1 yard (90cm) 12 inches = 1 foot (30cm) 1 inch (lin) = 2.5cm equivalents are as follows: Other English-speaking countries use the metric system. Approximate The lightest road bike ever weighs around 6 pounds.

It's 30 miles to the nearest hospital.

measures are also sometimes used. The  US generally uses non-metric units.

Metric measures are now standard for most other purposes, but non-metric We are now flying at an altitude of28,000feet.

The car park's straight on, about 500 yards on the right.

heights are often given in feet.

Britain uses not-ɪnetrie measures (miles, yards and feet) for road distances; 17 metric     and   non-metric       measures is a quarter.

five-cent coins are nickels; ten-cent coins are dimes; a twenty-five cent coin There are 100  cents (¢) in a dollar ($). One-cent coins are called pennies; 16 American      money vocabulary •  322   numbers For ways of saying and writing dates, ►    324.   For ways of telling the time, ►    325.
